content:
# Tasks Document

## Implementation Tasks

### Phase 1: Core Validation Logic

- [ ] 1. Add MONTHLY_LIMITS constant and validateMonthlyLimit() method
  - File: `backend/src/modules/claims/claims.controller.ts`
  - Add `private readonly MONTHLY_LIMITS` constant at class level (after constructor)
  - Implement `private async validateMonthlyLimit()` method following design specification
  - Method signature: `validateMonthlyLimit(userId: string, category: ClaimCategory, month: number, year: number, newAmount: number, excludeClaimId?: string): Promise<void>`
  - Logic: Check limit exists → fetch existing claims → filter excludeClaimId → sum amounts → validate total → throw 422 if exceeded
  - _Leverage: `ClaimDBUtil.getAll()`, existing exception patterns_
  - _Requirements: 1.1, 1.2_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec backend-claim-limit-validation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Backend developer implementing business validation logic | Task: Add MONTHLY_LIMITS constant and validateMonthlyLimit() private method to ClaimsController (lines after constructor and before existing validateClaimBusinessRules method). Use ClaimDBUtil.getAll() to fetch claims, Array.reduce() to sum totalAmount, throw UnprocessableEntityException if limit exceeded. Error message format: "TELCO monthly limit of $150.00 exceeded. Current: $120.00, Proposed: $50.00, Total: $170.00". Requirements 1.1 and 1.2 specify the validation logic and error messages. | Restrictions: Do not use raw SQL queries, use TypeORM via ClaimDBUtil. Do not add conditionals to skip validation. Do not use static keyword for MONTHLY_LIMITS. Follow existing error handling patterns in validateClaimBusinessRules(). | _Leverage: ClaimDBUtil.getAll() for fetching claims, existing UnprocessableEntityException pattern from validateClaimBusinessRules(), existing logger pattern this.logger.warn() | _Requirements: 1.1 (create claim validation), 1.2 (update claim validation), 3 (error messages) | Success: MONTHLY_LIMITS constant defined with { [ClaimCategory.TELCO]: 150, [ClaimCategory.FITNESS]: 50 }, validateMonthlyLimit() method implemented with correct signature, method fetches claims using ClaimDBUtil, sums amounts with reduce(), throws 422 with formatted message on limit exceeded, logs warnings on rejection | Instructions: After implementing, mark this task as in-progress in tasks.md by changing [ ] to [-], then mark as complete [x] when done_

- [ ] 2. Integrate validation into createClaim()
  - File: `backend/src/modules/claims/claims.controller.ts` (modify existing method)
  - Location: Line ~349, immediately after `this.validateClaimBusinessRules(createClaimDto);`
  - Add: `await this.validateMonthlyLimit(user.id, createClaimDto.category, createClaimDto.month, createClaimDto.year, createClaimDto.totalAmount);`
  - No conditionals - ALWAYS call validation
  - _Leverage: User object from @User() decorator, createClaimDto fields_
  - _Requirements: 1.1_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec backend-claim-limit-validation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Backend developer integrating validation into existing endpoints | Task: Add validateMonthlyLimit() call to createClaim() method immediately after validateClaimBusinessRules(). Pass user.id and all createClaimDto fields (category, month, year, totalAmount). No excludeClaimId parameter for create operations. Requirement 1.1 specifies validation must run on claim creation. | Restrictions: Do not add conditional checks before calling validation. Do not modify validateClaimBusinessRules(). Do not change error handling logic. Must call validation before claim creation (before claimDBUtil.create()). | _Leverage: Existing user object from @User() decorator, createClaimDto validated by NestJS ValidationPipe | _Requirements: 1.1 (create claim validation) | Success: validateMonthlyLimit() called in createClaim() after validateClaimBusinessRules(), all 5 parameters passed correctly (userId, category, month, year, totalAmount), no conditional logic around validation call, validation runs before claim entity creation | Instructions: After implementing, mark this task as in-progress in tasks.md by changing [ ] to [-], then mark as complete [x] when done_

- [ ] 3. Integrate validation into updateClaim()
  - File: `backend/src/modules/claims/claims.controller.ts` (modify existing method)
  - Location: Line ~545, immediately after `this.validateClaimBusinessRules(updateClaimDto);`
  - Add: `await this.validateMonthlyLimit(user.id, updateClaimDto.category ?? existingClaim.category, updateClaimDto.month ?? existingClaim.month, updateClaimDto.year ?? existingClaim.year, updateClaimDto.totalAmount ?? existingClaim.totalAmount, existingClaim.id);`
  - ALWAYS call validation - no conditionals checking if fields changed
  - _Leverage: existingClaim fetched earlier in method, updateClaimDto from request_
  - _Requirements: 1.2_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec backend-claim-limit-validation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Backend developer integrating validation into existing endpoints | Task: Add validateMonthlyLimit() call to updateClaim() method immediately after validateClaimBusinessRules(). Use nullish coalescing (??) to combine updateClaimDto values with existingClaim values (e.g., updateClaimDto.category ?? existingClaim.category). Pass existingClaim.id as excludeClaimId parameter. Requirement 1.2 specifies validation must run on claim updates to prevent limit circumvention. | Restrictions: Do not add conditional checks for "if fields changed" before calling validation. Always validate on update regardless of which fields changed. Do not modify validateClaimBusinessRules(). Must use nullish coalescing operator (??) not OR operator (||). | _Leverage: existingClaim entity already fetched in method (check line ~533), updateClaimDto validated by NestJS ValidationPipe | _Requirements: 1.2 (update claim validation), 2 (update edge cases) | Success: validateMonthlyLimit() called in updateClaim() after validateClaimBusinessRules(), all 6 parameters passed correctly using nullish coalescing for optional fields, existingClaim.id passed as excludeClaimId, no conditional logic around validation call, validation runs before claim update | Instructions: After implementing, mark this task as in-progress in tasks.md by changing [ ] to [-], then mark as complete [x] when done_

### Phase 2: Unit Testing

- [ ] 4. Write unit tests for validateMonthlyLimit()
  - File: `backend/src/modules/claims/claims.controller.spec.ts` (add new test suite)
  - Test scenarios: Telco limit exceeded, Fitness limit exceeded, under limit, unlimited category, update excludes current claim, empty existing claims, exact boundary
  - Mock ClaimDBUtil.getAll() to return test data
  - Verify UnprocessableEntityException thrown with correct message format
  - _Leverage: Existing test file structure, Vitest mocking patterns_
  - _Requirements: All requirements_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec backend-claim-limit-validation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: QA engineer writing comprehensive unit tests | Task: Create test suite for validateMonthlyLimit() in existing claims.controller.spec.ts file. Write 8 test scenarios: (1) Telco limit exceeded - mock $120 existing + $50 new = $170 > $150, expect 422; (2) Fitness limit exceeded - mock $40 existing + $15 new = $55 > $50, expect 422; (3) Telco under limit - mock $100 existing + $40 new = $140 < $150, expect success; (4) Unlimited category (Dental) - mock any amount, expect success; (5) Update excludes current claim - mock 2 claims ($80 + $60), update $60→$80, expect failure ($80+$80=$160>$150); (6) Update with no category change - mock claimName change only, expect validation called and success; (7) Empty existing claims - mock empty array, expect success; (8) Exact boundary - mock $100 + $50 = $150, expect success (not exceeding). Mock ClaimDBUtil.getAll(), verify exception messages match "TELCO monthly limit of $150.00 exceeded. Current: $120.00, Proposed: $50.00, Total: $170.00" format. | Restrictions: Must mock ClaimDBUtil, do not make real database calls. Test validation logic only, not database behavior. Verify exact error message format including 2 decimal places. Do not test NestJS framework code. | _Leverage: Existing test patterns in claims.controller.spec.ts, Vitest vi.fn() for mocking ClaimDBUtil methods | _Requirements: 1.1, 1.2, 3 (error messages), 5 (status inclusion) | Success: 8 unit tests written and passing, ClaimDBUtil properly mocked, all edge cases covered (limits exceeded, under limit, unlimited, updates, empty, boundary), error messages verified with correct format, tests isolated and fast (<100ms each) | Instructions: After implementing, mark this task as in-progress in tasks.md by changing [ ] to [-], then mark as complete [x] when done_

### Phase 3: Integration Testing

- [ ] 5. Write integration tests for limit enforcement
  - File: `api-test/src/tests/claims.test.ts` (add new test suite)
  - Test scenarios: Sequential claims enforce limits, different categories independent, different months independent, update revalidates, deleted claims don't count, category change revalidates
  - Use real database with clean state before each test
  - Verify HTTP 422 responses with correct error messages
  - _Leverage: Existing API test infrastructure, authenticated user context_
  - _Requirements: All requirements_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec backend-claim-limit-validation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: QA engineer writing API integration tests | Task: Create integration test suite in api-test/src/tests/claims.test.ts. Write 6 test scenarios: (1) Sequential creation - create Telco $100 (expect 201), create Telco $60 (expect 422 with error message); (2) Different categories - create Telco $150 (expect 201), create Fitness $50 (expect 201); (3) Different months - create Telco Sep $150 (expect 201), create Telco Oct $150 (expect 201); (4) Update revalidates - create Telco $80 + Telco $60 (both 201), update second to $80 (expect 422); (5) Deleted claims excluded - create Telco $100 (201), delete claim (204), create Telco $150 (expect 201); (6) Category change - create Dental $200 (201), update to Fitness category (expect 422). Verify 422 responses contain proper error messages. Clean database before each test. | Restrictions: Use real database transactions, do not mock API calls. Ensure clean state before each test (delete existing claims). Must test with authenticated user context. Verify exact HTTP status codes and error message format. | _Leverage: Existing API test setup in api-test/src/tests/claims.test.ts, authenticated user fixture, database cleanup utilities | _Requirements: 1.1, 1.2, 3 (error messages), 4 (concurrent handling note), 5 (deleted claims) | Success: 6 integration tests written and passing, tests run against real database with clean state, HTTP status codes verified (201 success, 422 limit exceeded, 204 delete), error messages verified in 422 responses, tests demonstrate cross-request state (sequential claims), all scenarios pass consistently | Instructions: After implementing, mark this task as in-progress in tasks.md by changing [ ] to [-], then mark as complete [x] when done_

### Phase 4: Documentation

- [ ] 6. Update OpenAPI/Swagger documentation
  - Files: `backend/src/modules/claims/claims.controller.ts` (modify decorators)
  - Add 422 response example to @ApiResponse decorators on createClaim() and updateClaim()
  - Response example: `{ "statusCode": 422, "message": "TELCO monthly limit of $150.00 exceeded. Current: $120.00, Proposed: $50.00, Total: $170.00", "error": "Unprocessable Entity" }`
  - Add JSDoc comments to validateMonthlyLimit() method
  - _Leverage: Existing @ApiResponse decorators, Swagger schema definitions_
  - _Requirements: 3 (error messages), NFR (usability)_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec backend-claim-limit-validation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Technical writer updating API documentation | Task: Add @ApiUnprocessableEntityResponse decorator to createClaim() (line ~297) and updateClaim() (line ~498) methods with 422 response example showing monthly limit exceeded error. Add JSDoc comment above validateMonthlyLimit() method describing purpose, parameters, thrown exceptions. Example response schema: statusCode 422, message with limit/current/proposed/total amounts, error "Unprocessable Entity". Requirement 3 specifies exact error message format. NFR usability requirement specifies API documentation must include 422 examples. | Restrictions: Do not modify existing @ApiResponse decorators for 200/201 responses. Follow existing Swagger decorator patterns. Ensure example message matches exact format from requirements. Must use @ApiUnprocessableEntityResponse decorator (not generic @ApiResponse). | _Leverage: Existing @ApiUnprocessableEntityResponse patterns in updateClaim() line ~498-510 for status transition errors, existing JSDoc patterns in controller | _Requirements: 3 (error messages), NFR Usability (API documentation) | Success: @ApiUnprocessableEntityResponse added to both createClaim() and updateClaim() with 422 example, JSDoc comment added to validateMonthlyLimit() with @param and @throws tags, Swagger UI displays 422 response examples correctly, documentation matches exact error message format from requirements | Instructions: After implementing, mark this task as in-progress in tasks.md by changing [ ] to [-], then mark as complete [x] when done_

### Phase 5: Database Optimization

- [ ] 7. Add database index for query performance
  - Create migration file for composite index
  - Index: `CREATE INDEX idx_claims_user_category_month_year ON claims(user_id, category, month, year) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;`
  - Partial index excludes soft-deleted claims for better performance
  - Test query performance with EXPLAIN ANALYZE
  - _Leverage: Existing TypeORM migration patterns_
  - _Requirements: NFR (performance)_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec backend-claim-limit-validation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Database administrator optimizing query performance | Task: Create TypeORM migration to add composite index on claims table for columns (user_id, category, month, year) with partial index condition WHERE deleted_at IS NULL. Use command "pnpm run migration:generate --name=AddClaimsLimitIndex" to generate migration, then edit migration file to add partial index. Test with EXPLAIN ANALYZE to verify index usage. NFR performance requirement specifies query optimization needed. | Restrictions: Must use TypeORM migration system, do not add index directly to database. Use partial index (WHERE deleted_at IS NULL) for better performance. Do not drop existing indexes. Verify index name doesn't conflict with existing indexes. | _Leverage: Existing migrations in backend/src/database/migrations/, TypeORM migration patterns, Makefile db/data/up command to run migrations | _Requirements: NFR Performance (query efficiency, <100ms validation target) | Success: Migration file created, composite index added on (user_id, category, month, year), partial index condition WHERE deleted_at IS NULL included, migration runs successfully with "make db/data/up", EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows index usage in ClaimDBUtil.getAll() queries, query performance <20ms for typical claim counts | Instructions: After implementing, mark this task as in-progress in tasks.md by changing [ ] to [-], then mark as complete [x] when done_

### Phase 6: Code Quality

- [ ] 8. Run formatting, linting, and tests
  - Run `make format` to format all code
  - Run `make lint` to check for linting errors
  - Run `make test/unit` to verify unit tests pass
  - Run `make test/api` to verify integration tests pass
  - Fix any errors or warnings
  - _Leverage: Project Makefile, ESLint/Prettier configs_
  - _Requirements: All_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec backend-claim-limit-validation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Quality assurance engineer running final checks | Task: Execute code quality checks in sequence: (1) make format - fixes code formatting, (2) make lint - checks for linting errors, (3) make test/unit - runs backend unit tests, (4) make test/api - runs API integration tests. Fix any errors reported by these commands. All requirements must be validated through passing tests. | Restrictions: Do not skip any quality checks. Must run from project root directory. Fix all linting errors before proceeding (warnings acceptable). All tests must pass (0 failures). Do not modify test expectations to make tests pass - fix implementation instead. | _Leverage: Makefile in project root, ESLint/Prettier configurations, Vitest test runner, existing test infrastructure | _Requirements: All requirements validated through passing tests | Success: make format completes without changes (code already formatted), make lint shows 0 errors (warnings OK), make test/unit shows all unit tests passing including new validateMonthlyLimit tests, make test/api shows all integration tests passing including new limit enforcement tests, no test failures or errors | Instructions: After implementing, mark this task as in-progress in tasks.md by changing [ ] to [-], then mark as complete [x] when done_

## Task Summary

**Total Tasks**: 8
**Estimated Effort**:
- Phase 1 (Core Logic): 4-6 hours
- Phase 2 (Unit Tests): 2-3 hours
- Phase 3 (Integration Tests): 2-3 hours
- Phase 4 (Documentation): 1 hour
- Phase 5 (Database): 1 hour
- Phase 6 (QA): 1 hour
**Total**: 11-15 hours

**Dependencies**:
- Tasks 2-3 depend on Task 1 (core method must exist first)
- Task 4 depends on Tasks 1-3 (tests validate implemented logic)
- Task 5 depends on Tasks 1-3 (integration tests need API changes)
- Task 6 can be done anytime after Task 1
- Task 7 can be done independently
- Task 8 must be last (validates everything)

**Risk Assessment**:
- **Low Risk**: Core logic is straightforward (fetch, filter, sum, compare)
- **Medium Risk**: Integration tests require careful database state management
- **Low Risk**: Documentation updates are simple additions
